issuesFound.title: missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. A descriptive page title is required for users to identify the purpose of the page and navigate between open tabs.

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: This button toggles the main navigation menu but lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 38: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: broken ARIA reference: element references id that does not exist

issuesFound.fix: Line 71: <ul id="resources-submenu" role="list" hidden>

issuesFound.title: non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Users navigating by links alone will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: Read more about our features (no credit card required)

issuesFound.fix: Line 170: <section class="logo-bar" aria-label="Trusted brands">

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides an outline-offset. Without a non-zero outline, box-shadow, or border, there is no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements that do not match :focus-visible. If a browser fails to support :focus-visible or incorrectly categorizes an interaction, the focus indicator is lost entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the focus indicator is set to outline: none. In high-contrast mode, box-shadows are often disabled, making a visible outline mandatory for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 327: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a replacement (like a box-shadow or border) within the same rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline. Keyboard users will not be able to visually track their position when navigating to the site home link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus indicator. This prevents keyboard users on mobile-width viewports from identifying the active element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the outline and only provide an outline-offset. Since the outline width is zero by default (due to the global reset), no indicator appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While it provides a box-shadow, box-shadows are often stripped in High Contrast Mode, leaving the element without an indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline for focus-visible states, which are specifically intended for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to verify they have reached the submit action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline, failing to provide a visible indicator for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the focus outline, which is a failure of SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus indicator, preventing keyboard users from knowing which category filter they are about to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle buttons remove the focus outline, creating a barrier for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1301: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table container is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable region is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1434: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus outline, making it difficult for keyboard users to navigate the list of questions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls remove the outline on focus. While a box-shadow is provided, it is insufficient for users in High Contrast Mode where shadows are removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1638: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, which is the primary indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1639: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 561: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.

issuesFound.explanation: The base button class has a min-height of 32px. While it meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px AAA recommendation and may be difficult to use without sufficient spacing.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle is sized at 28px, which is significantly smaller than the recommended 44px target for critical navigation elements.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size for form inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px by 28px, which is a small target for a primary search action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 734: width: 44px; Line 735: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1283: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product CTA buttons are restricted to a 28px min-height, making them harder to tap on mobile devices.

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class resets width/height to auto and overflow to visible. This fails to visually hide the content if the element contains text, and the lack of clipping means it may interfere with the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: width: 1px; Line 347: height: 1px; Line 350: overflow: hidden; Line 351: clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); clip-path: inset(50%); position: absolute;

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting word-spacing to -0.2em crowds text together, making it difficult for users with low vision or dyslexia to distinguish individual words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing (-0.05em) reduces the legibility of body text by causing characters to overlap or appear too close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly degrades readability for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but there is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes a transform transition. Users who are sensitive to motion may experience vestibular distress when buttons move on hover/active states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The arrow icon in feature card links translates horizontally on hover. This spatial movement lacks a reduced motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1000: .feature-card a::after { content: "→"; transition: none; display: inline-block; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion arrow rotates via a transition. While a media query exists on line 1588, it only targets the transition property, not the transform itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1588: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global reset removes underlines from all links. In body text (like .prose or paragraphs), this makes links indistinguishable from text for colorblind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #ffffff ratio 2.54:1
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text color (--colour-neutral-400) used in inputs against a white background fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio for informative text.

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.18:1
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker (.required-marker) uses --colour-error (#ee9999) against a white background, making the "required" signal unreadable for many users.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive feedback that the menu has been deployed or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`; Line 1461: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function in the `pricing` module updates the visual state of billing period buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify which billing cycle (monthly/annual) is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive); });`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error message and CSS classes but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3245: `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes the error message but does not reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to 'false'. The field will continue to be announced as invalid by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3240: `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger button. The initial state of the widget is ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `filterAnimation` module hides product cards using the `hidden` attribute but does not consistently apply `aria-hidden="true"`. This can lead to "ghost" elements remaining in the accessibility tree in some browser/screen reader combinations during transitions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: `toHide.forEach(el => { el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ...`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler changes the layout mode (grid vs list) and updates the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons, but does not announce the change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2340: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(`View changed to ${mode} layout`);`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` created in the `toastManager` uses the character '×' as its text content. Without a descriptive label, screen reader users will hear "times" or "multiplication sign" instead of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1315: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': STRINGS.labels.dismissToast }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module sets `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` on a `<div>` to create a drop zone but only handles the `keydown` event for Enter and Space. It does not ensure that the element is reachable and activatable in the same way as a native button for all keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: (The code currently handles keydown for Enter/Space, but ensure the click handler is also triggered) Line 4055: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); this.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `CONTACT_FIELDS` configuration for the contact form includes "Full name" and "Email address" but the module does not ensure the `autocomplete` attribute is present on these personal data fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3515: `const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl); if (field) { if (cfg.label === 'Full name') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); if (cfg.label === 'Email address') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules); }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `_toggleEl` (billing switch) in the `pricing` module acts as a controller for the price values in the cards, but it does not use `aria-controls` to programmatically associate the switch with the pricing section or cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2785: `if (_toggleEl) { const pricingGridId = srUtils.ensureId(qs('.pricing-grid'), 'pricing-grid'); setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CONTROLS, pricingGridId); ...`

issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but fail to update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive feedback that the menu has been deployed or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 1561: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch for month/year billing updates a visual class on the track but fails to update the `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked` state on the toggle element itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2321: `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` adds a visual error class and updates an error message but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3185: `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid` to `false` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3180: `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` state to `false`. This results in an undefined state for screen readers until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `filterAnimation.run` function uses the `hidden` attribute to hide product cards but does not consistently apply `aria-hidden="true"`. While `hidden` works in many browsers, `aria-hidden` is required for robust removal from the accessibility tree during animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField.bind` function adds a click listener to the `zoneEl` (a `div`) and sets `role="button"`, but it only handles `keydown` for Enter and Space. It does not ensure the element is treated as a button in all interaction contexts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3785: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keypress', (e) => { if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` created in the `toastManager` uses the character "×" as its text content. This is often announced as "times" or "multiplication sign" rather than "Close" or "Dismiss".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1415: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': STRINGS.labels.dismissToast || 'Dismiss' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The `CONTACT_FIELDS` configuration for the contact form includes "Full name" and "Email address" but the code does not ensure the `autocomplete` attribute is set on these inputs. This hinders users with cognitive disabilities who rely on browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3415: `if (field) { inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules); if (cfg.label.includes('Email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); if (cfg.label.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.init` function identifies the `_toggleEl` but does not set the `aria-controls` attribute to link it to the pricing cards or container it affects.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2305: `if (_toggleEl) { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-grid'); ... }`

Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
Missing autocomplete on personal data input

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `nav-open` class on the body and the `is-open` class on the drawer, but they never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has been deployed or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ...`
  Line 1561: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ...`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTabClick` function in the product filter controller updates the active category but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the filter buttons. Since these buttons act as toggles for the current view, their state must be programmatically available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: `function _onTabClick(e) { ... _productFilterState.activeCategory = value; qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === value))); ...`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` adds an error class and updates an error message element, but it does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field itself. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state when the user navigates to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: `function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { if (result.valid) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... } else { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... } }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger button. This leaves the initial state of the widget ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1618: `function init() { ... _initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller uses the `hidden` attribute to hide panels, but the CSS transition logic in `_closeInstance` delays the application of the attribute. During the transition, the element is visually fading but remains fully available in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1665: `function _openInstance(inst) { ... inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); ... }`
  Line 1678: `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user changes the sort order via the `_onSortChange` handler, the product grid is re-ordered and updated, but no announcement is made to screen readers that the sort has been applied or that the list order has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2265: `function _onSortChange(e) { ... applyFilters(); liveRegion.announce('Products sorted by ' + parts[0]); }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a `click` listener to the `zoneEl` (a `div`) to trigger the file input, but it does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key. Only the `Enter` key is handled, which violates the expectation that custom buttons respond to both keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3585: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form contains a "Full name" input field (`name="full_name"`) but does not include an `autocomplete` attribute. WCAG 2.1 requires that inputs collecting personal data have an appropriate autocomplete value to assist users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3285: `const nameField = qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl); if (nameField) nameField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion triggers are linked to their panels via `aria-controls` inside the `faqRegistry.parse` function, but if the panel does not have an ID, the reference is broken or missing. The code ensures an ID exists but does not verify the trigger's `aria-controls` attribute is set if it was missing from the source HTML.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755: `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', panelId);`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` in the dropdownMenu module opens a dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1525: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_renderResult` in the inlineValidator module marks a field as invalid by adding a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2674: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` inside the else block after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion init function sets initial open/closed state but does not set aria-expanded on trigger buttons for closed items. Screen reader users may not know the expandable state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, item.open);` inside the forEach loop after setting aria-expanded for open items.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_closeInstance` in the dropdownMenu module hides the panel by setting hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". Assistive technology may still access the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1543: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_updateStatus` in the siteSearch module updates a status element but that element is not an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed of search result counts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1449: Change the status element creation to include `'aria-live': 'polite'` and `role: 'status'` in its attributes.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function is attached to a div with role="switch" but the div does not have a keydown handler for Enter/Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1607: Add `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', _onToggleKeydown);` and define `_onToggleKeydown` to handle Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The function `togglePlay` in the testimonialsCarousel module toggles play/pause state but does not update aria-pressed on the play button. Screen reader users won't know the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2002: Add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing);` inside `togglePlay`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_updateCountBadge` in the productFilter module updates the product count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1913: Add `liveRegion.announce(count + ' products visible');` after updating the badge.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1161: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Since the menu is hidden by default, the initial state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader._onScroll` function toggles the `CSS.headerHidden` class on the header, changing its visibility, but does not announce this state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1548: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Header hidden');` (after adding class)

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function cycles the auto-play state but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the play/pause button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2458: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing);`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds the `CSS.fieldInvalid` class and updates the error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2688: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2683: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function changes the visibility of the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` (add this inside the `open` function)

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close` function uses `animateHeight` to hide the panel and sets the `hidden` attribute, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. This may leave the element partially discoverable in some AT modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1588: `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` (add this inside the `close` function)

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.goTo` function changes the active slide and updates the visual state, but it does not write to a live region. Users of screen readers will not know the slide has changed unless they manually navigate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselSlideAnnounce.replace('{n}', String(target + 1)).replace('{total}', String(_slides.length)));` (add this inside the `goTo` function)

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.play` function activates auto-play and updates the `aria-label` of the play button, but it does not update `aria-pressed`. The button's toggle state is not communicated to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1803: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');` (ensure this is called consistently in `play`)

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error text, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1987: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` (add this inside the `else` block of `_renderResult`)

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1983: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (add this inside the `if (result.valid)` block of `_renderResult`)

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer, but it does not ensure the trigger has an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the drawer's ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1198: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer));` (add this inside the `init` function)

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) and only adds outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for any interactive element. Keyboard‑only users cannot see focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the outline for elements that receive focus via mouse, leaving no visible focus style for any element that does not match :focus-visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible inside forced‑colors media
issuesFound.explanation: Within @media (forced-colors: active) the :focus-visible rule again sets outline: none with only an offset, removing the focus ring for high‑contrast mode users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 225: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: Small button variant has a min‑height of 20 px with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in a total touch target of ~32 px, below the WCAG 44 px recommended minimum. Affects users tapping small controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only button sets min‑width and min‑height of 20 px with 10 px padding, yielding a total target size of only 40 px, below the 44 px minimum for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width/height: auto, and clip: auto, which does not hide content from assistive technologies; it fails to provide a proper screen‑reader‑only technique.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses spacing between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non‑colour differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule “a { text-decoration: none; }” removes the default underline from all links, and no alternative visual cue (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img transitions its transform property on hover, creating movement, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disabling this animation, violating users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.fix: Line 560: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close change the drawer’s visibility but never set or clear aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button toggles the navigation drawer but never updates aria‑pressed, violating the ARIA button pattern for a two‑state control.
issuesFound.fix: In the click handler, after opening or closing, call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open ? 'true' : 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not reference the drawer via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance adds the “open” class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, so assistive tech cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance removes the “open” class but does not set aria‑expanded to false on the trigger, leaving the state stale for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via setAttribute('hidden') but aria‑hidden is never set, so the element may remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In _dropdownMenu._closeInstance add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in _openInstance remove aria‑hidden.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open expands the answer panel but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close collapses the panel without clearing aria‑expanded on the trigger, leaving AT with incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via panel.setAttribute('hidden') but aria‑hidden is never applied, so the content stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.close add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and in open remove aria‑hidden.

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion list toggle does not manage aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open and siteSearch._close show/hide the suggestion list but never set aria‑expanded on the search input, so screen readers cannot tell if suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch._open add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _close add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion list hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The list is hidden via setAttribute('hidden') but aria‑hidden is never set, leaving the list in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In suggestionListRenderer.hide add setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and in show remove aria‑hidden.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: Validation results are reflected only by adding error‑message elements; aria‑invalid is never set, so AT cannot detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate when result.valid is false call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when true remove or set to false.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, aria‑invalid remains set, causing persistent error state for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In the same validation path, when result.valid is true call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid').

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The badge text changes to reflect the number of matching products, but no live‑region is used, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> and update its textContent whenever the count changes.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step changes are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the wizard moves to a new step, the visible change is not announced via a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the step transition.
issuesFound.fix: After changing steps, set textContent of a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> to a message like “Step X of Y: <step title>”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch for billing period changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed, violating the ARIA switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing._onToggleClick add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true' ? 'false' : 'true').

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not set aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The switch controls the pricing table but lacks aria‑controls linking to the controlled region, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.init add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'pricing-table')).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the overlay is hidden, it is only removed from the DOM visually; aria‑hidden remains unset, so the overlay may stay in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav._unlockScroll add setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when hiding and remove when showing.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Assistive technologies therefore cannot know that the navigation drawer is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not reset aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` removes CSS classes but never sets `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the button in an incorrect state for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` adds the `open` class to the panel but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the associated button, so the expanded state is not exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close routine does not clear aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` removes the `open` class but leaves `aria‑expanded` on the button unchanged, causing a mismatch between visual and accessibility state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack an initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse()` the trigger button receives `aria-controls` but never gets an explicit `aria‑expanded` attribute (default should be `false`). Without it, screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the answer panel is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not receive aria‑expanded when the suggestion list opens
issuesFound.explanation: `siteSearch._mount()` creates the listbox and sets `aria-controls`, `aria-autocomplete`, etc., but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the `<input>`. AT therefore cannot detect that a dropdown is now visible.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not reset aria‑expanded when the suggestion list closes
issuesFound.explanation: `siteSearch._close()` hides the listbox but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the input, leaving the expanded state stale for assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed when selected
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a period button toggles visual classes but never updates `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot know which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch does not expose its state via aria‑checked**
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives `aria‑checked`, leaving the binary state invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Pricing sync routine does not keep aria‑pressed in sync for period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: `_syncToggleState()` adds/removes CSS classes on period buttons but never updates `aria‑pressed`, causing a mismatch between visual and accessibility state after programmatic changes (e.g., on page load).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter updates do not announce result count to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the UI updates the count badge but never announces the new number via a live region, leaving users of AT unaware of how many products are shown.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not set aria‑expanded when opened via keyboard navigation
issuesFound.explanation: When the user focuses the search input and presses Enter or ArrowDown, the suggestion panel becomes visible, but `aria‑expanded` remains `false`. This breaks the relationship between the combobox and its popup for AT.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid without this attribute.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state to open but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not know the answer is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Users relying on assistive technology will not be informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers will incorrectly report the menu as expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the drawer visibility but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the hamburger trigger. Assistive technology users will not know the menu state changed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The trigger will incorrectly announce as expanded to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle sync fails to update aria-pressed or aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates visual classes for billing period buttons but does not update aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-checked (for switches). The state change is not conveyed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge function updates the visible product count but does not announce the change via a live region. Users are not informed when the number of results changes.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and UI but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual") via a live region. Users are unaware the context has changed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu registration does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is undefined for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up listeners but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The default state is not communicated to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance fails to set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function sets the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel remains in the accessibility tree despite being visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close fails to set aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes visibility classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. The navigation content remains accessible to screen readers when closed.

issuesFound.title: Site search open function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function displays the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input (combobox). Screen readers will not announce the list is open.

issuesFound.title: Site search close function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._close function hides the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input. The combobox state is incorrectly reported as expanded.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top click action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page but does not announce the action (e.g., "Scrolled to top") via a live region. Users are not confirmed the action completed.

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal animation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The viewportReveal._reveal function makes content visible dynamically but does not announce the new content via a live region. Users may miss content appearing below the fold.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation links update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The activeNavLinks.init function updates aria-current="page" dynamically based on URL but does not announce the change in active page context via a live region.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes on the drawer and body, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the menu to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2815: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` in the open function.
  Line 2840: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, the trigger button (_trigger) does not have aria-expanded set to "false" to reflect that the menu is initially closed. Assistive technologies may not recognize the button as controlling an expandable region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2885: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after verifying `_trigger` exists and before attaching event listeners.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the modal overlay. Additionally, focus is not returned to the trigger button when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2818: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` in the open function after showing the drawer.
  Line 2842: Add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }` in the close function before unlocking scroll.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows and hides panels via CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but does not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons (inst.trigger). Screen readers cannot convey whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3035: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _openInstance after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.
  Line 3062: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _closeInstance after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register function missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup but does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` in the register function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input acts as a combobox. The _open and _close functions show and hide the suggestion listbox but do not update aria-expanded on the input element to reflect the listbox visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3635: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the _open function.
  Line 3670: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the _close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch mount function missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the search combobox is mounted, the input element is given role="combobox" and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3405: Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` in the mount function.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly/annual/quarterly) function as toggle buttons where one is always active. The code updates CSS classes to show the active state but does not set aria-pressed on the buttons to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4490: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop in _syncToggleState.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the _renderResult function adds error styling and updates aria-describedby via srUtils.markInvalid, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. When valid, it does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4995 (invalid case): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`.
  Line 5005 (valid case): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4460: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);` after updating all plan cards.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ panels are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers, and focus is not moved to the panel content. Users may not realize the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4200: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` in the open function.
  Line 4245: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, the drawer is hidden via CSS classes but aria-hidden="true" is not set on the _drawer element. While CSS may hide it, explicitly setting aria-hidden ensures it is definitely removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2845: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close function after removing the open class.
  Line 2818: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` in the open function to ensure it's visible when open.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel missing aria-live on slide change (alternative to existing announcement)
issuesFound.explanation: While the carousel updates aria-hidden and tabindex on slides, it relies on the liveRegion.announce for slide changes. However, the slide container itself should have aria-live="polite" to ensure changes are announced even if the liveRegion utility fails or is delayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4710: Ensure the carousel root element (_rootEl) has `setAttr(_rootEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` and `setAttr(_rootEl, 'aria-atomic', 'false');` during initialization, or verify the existing liveRegion announcement is sufficient. (Note: The existing liveRegion announcement at line 4740 is present, but marking the carousel region itself provides redundancy.)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not set aria-expanded to reflect the open/closed state of their associated panels. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line _closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to reflect the validation state. Screen readers cannot convey whether a field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _renderResult() valid block: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`)
  Line _renderResult() invalid block: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);`)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Assistive technologies may assume the menu is already expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): After `_initialised = true;` and before adding event listeners, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to miss the initial collapsed state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line register(): After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element, preventing assistive technologies from identifying the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): After ensuring _drawer has an id (e.g., `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');`), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is not removed from the accessibility tree via aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may still navigate to hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line close(): Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` at the start of the function
  Line open(): Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');` (or `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`) after opening

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _updateCountBadge(): After `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates the visual prices but does not announce the change via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed of the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod(): After `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action completion to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line _onClick(): After `window.scrollTo(...)` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement, so screen reader users are not notified when menu content appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance(): After `emit(...)` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line _closeInstance(): After `emit(...)` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but does not have aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element. <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion-style section triggers in the mobile navigation have aria-controls but lack aria-expanded to indicate whether the subsection is visible. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the current state of the disclosure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 980: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element. <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle button dynamically changes its aria-label based on state but does not expose the expanded state via aria-expanded. Assistive technology cannot programmatically determine whether the navigation panel is currently open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element. <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button renders only an emoji icon without an aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text. Although the IconButton interface requires aria-label, the component invocation omits it, leaving screen reader users without any accessible name for the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: Add aria-label prop to IconButton. <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle"> {ICONS[theme]} </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button contains only the "‹" character with no aria-label or other accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose or direction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton. <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev"> ‹ </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button contains only the "›" character with no aria-label or other accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose or direction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton. <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next"> › </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify when the input contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: Add aria-invalid to the input element. <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.fix: Line 740: Add aria-invalid to the textarea element. <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so the error message renders without an id attribute. This creates a broken reference that prevents screen readers from announcing error messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add errorId="newsletter-email-error" to FormGroup props. <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Add errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup props. <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Section component ignores labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but does not apply it to the rendered element as aria-labelledby. Landmarks using this component cannot be programmatically associated with their headings, reducing navigability for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: Add aria-labelledby={labelledby} to the Tag element. <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}> {children} </Tag>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state, it renders a spinner and loading text but does not set aria-busy to indicate that the element is being updated. Screen readers may not convey the loading state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 680: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element. <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button spinner not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.fix: Line 685: Add aria-hidden="true" to the spinner span. <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★ and ☆) are decorative content inside a container that already provides an aria-label with the full rating text. These characters lack aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "black star" or "white star" redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 570: Add aria-hidden="true" to the star spans. {stars.map((filled, i) => ( {filled ? '★' : '☆'} ))}

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the modal overlay div instead of the dialog div with role="dialog". This causes assistive technology to incorrectly treat the backdrop as the modal root, potentially allowing escape from the modal focus trap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Move aria-modal="true" from the overlay div to the dialog div. <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel uses role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", failing to indicate to assistive technology that the content underneath is inert and that focus should be trapped within the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div. <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos used for the marquee animation is not marked with aria-hidden. Screen readers will announce the integration partners twice, creating a repetitive and confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: Add aria-hidden="true" to the cloned ul element. <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">
